data.title: Sweden's Green Steelmaker Stegra Seeks $2 Billion for Project Completion
data.description: Sweden's green transition faces challenges as Stegra, a green steelmaker, announces it requires over $2 billion to finalize its flagship project. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the financial hurdles Stegra encounters in advancing its sustainable steel production initiative. The company aims to revolutionize the steel industry by reducing carbon emissions, but securing the necessary funding remains a significant obstacle. Stegra's project is part of Sweden's broader efforts to shift towards environmentally friendly industrial practices. The need for substantial investment underscores the complexities involved in transitioning to green technologies, which are crucial for achieving climate goals. As Stegra seeks financial backing, the situation reflects the broader challenges faced by industries worldwide in balancing economic viability with environmental sustainability.

data.title: Strategy CEO Sells Shares Amid SEC Filing
data.description: Strategy's CEO Phong Le has sold 2,034 shares, according to Odaily. The transaction was disclosed in a recent Form 144 filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The shares were sold at an average price of $137.25 each, totaling approximately $279,200.

data.title: AI Startups Attract Intense Investment Competition
data.description:
The race to invest in promising AI startups has intensified, with venture capitalists facing challenges in securing their desired stakes. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the fierce competition among investors eager to capitalize on the burgeoning artificial intelligence sector. 

As AI technology continues to advance, startups in this field are attracting significant attention and funding. However, the high demand has led to a competitive environment where not all venture capitalists can secure the investments they seek. This trend underscores the growing importance and potential of AI innovations in the market.

The surge in interest is driven by the transformative impact AI is expected to have across various industries, prompting investors to seek early involvement in promising ventures. Despite the challenges, the enthusiasm for AI startups remains strong, reflecting the sector's potential for substantial growth and innovation.

data.title: Raajmarg Infra Investment Trust's IPO Sees Strong Demand
data.description: Raajmarg Infra Investment Trust's initial public offering, valued at 60 billion rupees ($649 million), was oversubscribed four times on the last day of bidding. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the robust interest in yield-focused assets despite recent market fluctuations. This strong demand underscores investor confidence in infrastructure investments, even as markets experience volatility.

data.title: Retail Investors Show Persistent Fatigue, Says JPM
data.description: Macro Charts posted on X, highlighting JPM's analysis on retail investor flows. Weekly purchases have dropped 30%, and ETF inflows have decreased by 22%. This marks the first time this year that retail investors are showing persistent signs of weakness.

data.title: Indonesia Struggles to Keep Budget Deficit Within Legal Limits Amid Rising Oil Prices
data.description: Indonesia is encountering challenges in adhering to its budget deficit legal limit of 3% of gross domestic product due to escalating oil prices driven by tensions in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of these geopolitical issues on Indonesia's economic stability. The surge in oil prices has surpassed initial forecasts, complicating the government's efforts to manage fiscal policies effectively. As the situation unfolds, Indonesia's financial authorities are under pressure to find solutions to mitigate the economic strain caused by external factors. The government is exploring various strategies to address the fiscal challenges while maintaining economic growth and stability.

data.title: Binance Launches Velvet Trading Competition with Token Rewards
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to introduce the Velvet Trading Competition on Binance Alpha, offering exclusive token rewards to participants. The competition will be divided into two promotion periods: the first from 2026-03-13 13:00 (UTC) to 2026-03-20 13:00 (UTC), and the second from 2026-03-20 13:00 (UTC) to 2026-03-27 13:00 (UTC). Participants will be ranked based on their total purchase volume of Velvet (VELVET) tokens during each period. The top 3,370 users by purchase volume will share a total of 1,314,300 VELVET tokens equally, with each eligible user receiving 390 VELVET tokens.

Eligibility and Participation: To qualify, trades must be executed via Binance Wallet (Keyless) or Binance Alpha, excluding third-party dApp transactions. Only cumulative purchases count, and selling is excluded from the competition. There is no cap on trading volume for participants, and token bridging transactions are not eligible. Effective from 2025-06-17 00:00 (UTC), trading volume from Alpha-to-Alpha token pairs will not count towards Alpha Points or impact competition rankings. Rewards will be claimable in VELVET tokens before 2026-04-10 13:00:00 (UTC), and eligible users must claim within 14 days to avoid forfeiture.

Terms and Conditions: Participants must maintain an active Binance Account to be eligible. Binance reserves the right to disqualify users who breach terms or interfere with the program. The platform may amend or cancel the activity at its discretion. Users are advised to review the Terms of Use and Risk Warning, as Binance Alpha assets are subject to increased volatility and risks. Binance will not be liable for any losses incurred from investments in Alpha Assets.

data.title: AI Trends | Scientists Leverage AI to Tackle Climate Research Challenges
data.description: Scientists are increasingly utilizing artificial intelligence to address persistent questions in climate research. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how AI is being employed to analyze complex climate data and improve predictive models. This approach aims to enhance the understanding of climate change impacts and inform policy decisions. Researchers believe that AI can offer new insights by processing vast amounts of data more efficiently than traditional methods. The integration of AI in climate science is seen as a promising development in the ongoing efforts to combat climate change.

data.title: Strategy CEO sells 3,034 shares of company stock to raise cash.
data.description: According to a recent Form 144 filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, Phong Le, CEO of Bitcoin treasury company Strategy, has sold 2,034 shares at an average price of $137.25 per share, netting approximately $279,200. (BTC Treasuries)

data.title: Both WTI and Brent crude oil prices fell briefly, with WTI crude currently trading at $93.45 per barrel.
data.description: According to Gate data, both WTI and Brent crude oil prices fell in the short term. WTI crude oil dropped 3.00% intraday, currently trading at $93.45 per barrel. Brent crude oil is currently down 2.2%, trading at $96.74 per barrel.

data.title: Cuban President Engages in Dialogue with U.S. Government
data.description: Cuban President has announced that state officials have initiated discussions with the U.S. government. According to Jin10, the aim of these talks is to address and resolve issues through dialogue. This development marks a significant step in diplomatic relations between the two countries, highlighting a potential shift towards more cooperative engagement. The discussions are expected to focus on various bilateral concerns, although specific topics have not been disclosed. The move comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and could pave the way for improved relations.

data.title: Ukraine and Sweden Strengthen Military Partnership
data.description: Ukraine's Armed Forces Commander held a significant meeting with Sweden's Armed Forces Commander, General Mikael Claesson. According to Jin10, the visit underscores Sweden's strategic priority in maintaining its partnership with Ukraine.

data.title: UK Manufacturers Urge Approval for Jackdaw and Rosebank Oil and Gas Projects
data.description: Manufacturers in the United Kingdom are calling for the government to approve the Jackdaw and Rosebank oil and gas field projects. According to Jin10, these projects are seen as crucial for bolstering the country's energy security and supporting the manufacturing sector. The call for approval comes amid ongoing discussions about the UK's energy strategy and its commitment to reducing carbon emissions. Industry leaders argue that these projects could provide a stable energy supply while the country transitions to more sustainable energy sources. The decision on these projects is expected to have significant implications for the UK's energy landscape and economic stability.

data.title: Germany Criticizes Hungary's Pipeline Excuse
data.description: Germany's Foreign Ministry has expressed disapproval of Hungary's use of pipeline issues as an excuse. According to Jin10, the German spokesperson stated that Hungary's actions are unacceptable. This criticism comes amid ongoing tensions between the two countries over energy policies and infrastructure. The German government is urging Hungary to address the matter responsibly and avoid using it as a pretext for political maneuvering. The situation highlights the broader challenges within the European Union regarding energy security and cooperation.

data.title: Germany Stresses Continued Pressure on Russia
data.description: Germany has emphasized the necessity of maintaining pressure on Russia, according to Jin10. The German government spokesperson highlighted the importance of sustained efforts to address ongoing geopolitical tensions. This stance reflects Germany's commitment to international diplomatic strategies aimed at resolving conflicts and ensuring stability in the region.

data.title: The National Cybersecurity Notification Center issued a security risk warning for OpenClaw.
data.description: Data from the National Cybersecurity Information Notification Center shows that there are currently over 200,000 active OpenClaw internet assets globally, with approximately 23,000 active within China, exhibiting explosive growth. These assets are mainly concentrated in internet-rich regions such as Beijing, Shanghai, Guangdong, Zhejiang, Sichuan, and Jiangsu. The large number of OpenClaw assets exposed to the internet poses significant security risks and makes them highly vulnerable to cyberattacks. In particular, the uncontrollable behavior of OpenClaw agents makes them difficult to manage. OpenClaw agents are prone to losing control during instruction execution, leading to unauthorized task execution and disregard for user instructions. This could result in the deletion of user data, the theft of user information, and takeover of user terminal devices, causing significant economic losses. (Jinshi)

data.title: Swiss Central Bank Likely to Maintain Zero Policy Rate Amid Rising Oil Prices
data.description: Economist Andrew Kenningham from Capital Economics has indicated that the Swiss National Bank is expected to keep its policy rate at zero next week, despite the rising oil and gas prices and their impact on inflation. According to Jin10, Kenningham noted that the transmission effect of energy prices on inflation is likely to be moderate, and the upward pressure on the Swiss franc will help mitigate any inflationary impact. This situation suggests that the threshold for a rate cut is high, while the threshold for a rate hike is even higher compared to the European Central Bank. He further explained that amid the increasing risk of rate hikes by the European Central Bank, the Swiss National Bank can maintain a differential of approximately 200 basis points without lowering its policy rate.

data.title: U.S. Military Transports Injured Soldiers from Saudi Arabia to Germany
data.description: On March 12, the U.S. military transported 19 injured soldiers from Saudi Arabia to Germany for medical treatment. According to Jin10, a military aircraft departed from Saudi Arabia, made a stop in Oman, and then arrived at a U.S. military base in Germany. The base is located near a military hospital where the soldiers will receive care.

data.title: The whale that bought 2.2 million Trump tokens today previously suffered a huge loss of $14.61 million on Melania.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, a whale who lost $14.61 million on MELANIA in February last year has bought 2.2 million TRUMP tokens ($6.97 million) today in preparation for attending a conference: 1. In early February last year, he spent 28.93 million USDC to buy 13.975 million MELANIA tokens, with an average cost of $2.07. 2. At the end of February, he sold all his holdings at a price of $1.02, incurring a loss of $14.61 million (-50%). 3. Today, he bought 2.2 million TRUMP tokens ($6.97 million) at a price of $3.17.

data.title: Tether plans to complete a full audit by 2026, with a valuation target of $500 billion.
data.description: Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino stated that the company plans to complete a full audit by the end of 2026 and is currently in discussions with the Big Four accounting firms. Tether disclosed that its profits exceeded $10 billion last year and currently holds approximately $122 billion in U.S. Treasury bonds, making it the 17th largest holder of U.S. Treasury bonds globally. Furthermore, Tether is promoting its compliant token, USAT, in the U.S. market and has made over 140 investments in areas such as artificial intelligence, energy, and brain-computer interfaces. Tether is also currently attracting investors globally, attempting to establish a company valuation of $500 billion, making it one of the world's most valuable private companies. (Bloomberg)
